Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Researchers find 3,500-year-old loom that reveals textile revolution 49 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. William Blake, Remote by the Sea 40 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Agents of Chaos 43 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Cherri – programming language that compiles to an Apple Shortuct 221 upvotes, 45 comments
  5. Do your own writing 293 upvotes, 88 comments
  6. Fedware: Government apps that spy harder than the apps they ban 335 upvotes, 103 comments
  7. How to turn anything into a router 549 upvotes, 202 comments
  8. Roulette Computers: Hidden Devices That Predict Spins 47 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 189 upvotes, 74 comments
  10. Show HN: 30u30.fyi – Is your startup founder on Forbes' most fraudulent list? 155 upvotes, 59 comments
  11. Seeing Like a Spreadsheet 59 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. Recover Apple Keychain 45 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 106 upvotes, 44 comments
  14. Take better notes, by hand 164 upvotes, 77 comments
  15. Turning a MacBook into a touchscreen with $1 of hardware (2018) 140 upvotes, 67 comments
  16. The Digital Leviathan 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. FTC action against Match and OkCupid for deceiving users, sharing personal data 223 upvotes, 114 comments
  18. Show HN: Coasts – Containerized Hosts for Agents 49 upvotes, 20 comments
  19. A sea of sparks: Seeing radioactivity 41 upvotes, 16 comments
  20. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 281 upvotes, 166 comments
  21. IronGlass Brings Legendary Soviet Cinema Lenses to Mirrorless Cameras 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Learn Claude Code by doing, not reading 92 upvotes, 48 comments
  23. Bird brains (2023) 278 upvotes, 181 comments
  24. OCR for construction documents does not work, we fixed it 112 upvotes, 72 comments
  25. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 236 upvotes, 184 comments
  26. Why I'm betting on ATProto (and why you should, too) 12 upvotes, 4 comments
  27. Android Developer Verification 57 upvotes, 38 comments
  28. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in our office lab 69 upvotes, 55 comments
  29. Tickets Are Prompts 13 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. In math, rigor is vital, but are digitized proofs taking it too far? 91 upvotes, 80 comments

Last generated 7:30am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
